blurb: The earth was subjected to us, yet we were subjected to futility—until the One who made us stooped lower than our failure. These lines hold the ache and the answer together: our brief, wounded life cradled inside the eternal Son’s decision to taste our dust. Let them steady you today; everything that feels too heavy for human shoulders has already been carried by hands that shaped the stars.

verses.text: For unto the angels hath he not put in subjection the world to come, whereof we speak.
But one in a certain place testified, saying, What is man, that thou art mindful of him? or the son of man that thou visitest him?
But we see Jesus, who was made a little lower than the angels for the suffering of death, crowned with glory and honour; that he by the grace of God should taste death for every man.
